task_completion.description: The agent opens the email client to view the inbox.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email client (e.g., Thunderbird) is open and displaying the inbox with multiple emails.

task_completion.description: The agent creates and saves the contact list file in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file manager shows a file named 'contact_list.txt' saved in the '~/Documents' (or /home/ga/Documents) directory.

task_completion.description: The contact list contains at least 15 unique email addresses, one per line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'contact_list.txt' are visible in a text editor, showing a vertical list of at least 15 distinct email addresses with no other text.

task_completion.description: The agent starts a draft email to the correct recipient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email composition window is open with the 'To:' field set exactly to 'meetup-announce@techgroup.org'.

task_completion.description: The draft email has the correct subject line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Subject:' field in the email composition window is set exactly to 'Upcoming Tech Meetup Announcement'.

task_completion.description: The draft email body mentions that the contact list has been compiled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The body of the draft email contains a message notifying the recipient about the contact list (look for keywords like 'contact', 'list', 'compiled', 'meetup', or 'event').

integrity.description: The draft email is composed in an actual graphical email client.
integrity.visual_evidence: The draft is seen in an email client's composition window (like Thunderbird), not just written as a raw text file or terminal command.

integrity.description: The agent extracted real emails from the inbox rather than hallucinating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The email addresses in 'contact_list.txt' look like genuine addresses from a corpus, not generic placeholders like 'email1@test.com' or 'person@example.com'.

integrity.description: The agent used GUI tools to complete the task rather than terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the email client and a text editor, rather than terminal windows running commands like 'grep' or 'python' to parse the raw mail files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be exactly at '/home/ga/Documents/contact_list.txt' and contain at least 15 unique email addresses (one per line). The draft email must be addressed to 'meetup-announce@techgroup.org' with the subject 'Upcoming Tech Meetup Announcement'. The draft body must contain at least 2 of the following keywords: 'meetup', 'event', 'contact', 'list', 'compiled'.
